title: IISc Administrative Assistant Guess Paper 7
subtitle: Hard-tier prediction set modelled on 2023 examiner patterns
instructions: Total 80 questions to be attempted in 90 minutes.
Negative marking applies: 0.33333 marks (one-third) are deducted for every incorrect answer.
Figure-based placeholder questions are unscored and carry 0 marks either way.
Difficulty is benchmarked at or above the actual 2023 paper: expect multi-step questions.

questions.text: In an examination, 34% of candidates failed in Mathematics, 42% failed in English and 20% failed in both. The percentage of candidates who passed in both subjects is:
questions.explanation: Failed in at least one = 34 + 42 - 20 = 56; passed both = 44.

questions.text: A man buys an article and sells it at 20% profit. Had he bought it for 20% less and sold it for Rs 75 less, he would have gained 25%. The original cost price is:
The single discount equivalent to successive discounts of 20%, 15% and 10% is:
4 men and 6 women finish a work in 8 days, while 3 men and 7 women finish it in 10 days. In how many days will 10 women alone finish it?
questions.explanation: 1 man = 11 women; total work = 400 woman-days.

questions.text: Pipes A (fills in 12 h) and B (fills in 15 h) are opened at 8 a.m. Pipe C, which empties the tank in 20 h, is opened at 10 a.m. At what time will the tank be full?
questions.explanation: 3/10 done by 10 a.m.; the rest at 1/10 per hour takes 7 hours.

questions.text: A policeman spots a thief 100 m away. The thief runs at 8 km/h and the policeman chases at 10 km/h. Enter the distance (in metres) the thief runs before being caught.
questions.explanation: Gap closes at 2 km/h in 3 minutes; thief covers 8 x 0.05 km.

questions.text: A man rows to a place 48 km away and returns in 14 hours. He finds he can row 4 km with the stream in the same time as 3 km against it. The speed of the stream is:
questions.explanation: Down:up = 4:3; 48/4k + 48/3k = 14 gives k = 2; stream = 1.

questions.text: A sum becomes 1331/1000 of itself in 3 years at compound interest. The annual rate is:
The incomes of A and B are in the ratio 9:7 and their expenditures in the ratio 4:3. If each saves Rs 2000, A's income is:
questions.explanation: 9x - 4y = 7x - 3y = 2000 gives x = 2000; A earns 18000.

questions.text: In what ratio must water be mixed with milk costing Rs 60 per litre so that the mixture is worth Rs 48 per litre?
The sum of three consecutive odd numbers is 20 more than the first of these numbers. The middle number is:
questions.explanation: 2x + 6 = 20 gives x = 7; middle = 9.

questions.text: If a - b = 3 and a^2 + b^2 = 29, then ab equals:
A invests Rs 12000 at the start of the year. B joins after 3 months with a capital X. At year end the profits are shared equally. X equals:
A rectangular sheet 44 cm x 20 cm is rolled along its length to form a cylinder. The volume of the cylinder is: (use pi = 22/7)
questions.explanation: 2 pi r = 44 gives r = 7; V = pi x 49 x 20 = 3080.

questions.text: The area enclosed by the graph |x| + |y| = 4 is:
questions.explanation: A square with diagonals 8 and 8: area = 32.

questions.text: A chord subtends an angle of 60 degrees at the centre of a circle of radius 12 cm. The length of the chord is:
questions.explanation: Chord = 2r sin(30) = 12; the triangle is equilateral.

questions.text: Sentence 1: Antibiotic resistance is now a leading global health threat. Arrange P, Q, R, S. P: Bacteria exposed to incomplete doses survive and pass on their defences. Q: Patients often stop their course the moment symptoms fade. R: The result is infections that no common drug can cure. S: Doctors therefore insist that every prescribed course be completed.
questions.explanation: Habit, mechanism, consequence, medical advice.

questions.text: Sentence 1: The Silk Road was never a single road. Arrange P, Q, R, S. P: Along them travelled not just silk but religions, technologies and diseases. Q: It was a shifting web of caravan tracks linking China to the Mediterranean. R: Its true legacy is therefore cultural as much as commercial. S: Buddhism reached China and paper reached Europe by these routes.
questions.explanation: Definition, what travelled, examples, conclusion.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: To pour oil on troubled waters

options.text: To make a bad situation worse
To calm a quarrel or dispute

questions.explanation: It means to soothe a heated situation.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: To gird up one's loins

options.text: To prepare oneself for a difficult task

questions.explanation: It means to get ready for demanding work.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the phrase: A white elephant
questions.explanation: It is something expensive to keep yet of little use.

questions.text: Government by officials:
questions.explanation: Bureaucracy is rule by administrative officials.

questions.text: A speech delivered without any previous preparation:
questions.explanation: An extempore speech is unprepared.

questions.text: Select the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: Perseverance ends in -ance.

questions.text: Select the incorrectly spelt word:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is dilemma.

questions.text: Improve the underlined part: Scarcely had he entered THAN the phone rang.
questions.explanation: 'Scarcely/hardly' pairs with 'when', not 'than'.

questions.text: Improve the underlined part: The number of road accidents HAVE increased sharply this year.
questions.explanation: 'The number of' takes a singular verb.

questions.text: Select the synonym of TACITURN:
questions.explanation: Taciturn means habitually silent.

questions.text: Select the antonym of LUCID:
questions.explanation: Lucid (clear) is opposite to obscure.

questions.passage: Platform work promises freedom: log in when you like, work as much as you choose. Millions of delivery riders and cab drivers have taken up the offer, yet studies reveal a more complicated bargain. Algorithms assign tasks, set pay and rate performance, exercising a control tighter than many formal employers, while the costs of vehicles, fuel and insurance fall on the worker. Because platforms classify them as independent partners rather than employees, most gig workers lack sick leave, pensions and minimum-wage protection. Several countries are now testing a third legal category, between employee and contractor, to extend basic security without erasing flexibility.
questions.text: The central paradox the passage highlights is that gig work:

options.text: Advertises freedom while algorithms exert tight control

questions.explanation: Freedom is promised, yet algorithmic control is tighter than many employers'.

questions.passage: Platform work promises freedom: log in when you like, work as much as you choose. Millions of delivery riders and cab drivers have taken up the offer, yet studies reveal a more complicated bargain. Algorithms assign tasks, set pay and rate performance, exercising a control tighter than many formal employers, while the costs of vehicles, fuel and insurance fall on the worker. Because platforms classify them as independent partners rather than employees, most gig workers lack sick leave, pensions and minimum-wage protection. Several countries are now testing a third legal category, between employee and contractor, to extend basic security without erasing flexibility.
questions.text: According to the passage, gig workers lack sick leave and pensions because:

options.text: They are classified as independent partners, not employees

questions.explanation: The classification outside employment excludes them from protections.

questions.passage: Platform work promises freedom: log in when you like, work as much as you choose. Millions of delivery riders and cab drivers have taken up the offer, yet studies reveal a more complicated bargain. Algorithms assign tasks, set pay and rate performance, exercising a control tighter than many formal employers, while the costs of vehicles, fuel and insurance fall on the worker. Because platforms classify them as independent partners rather than employees, most gig workers lack sick leave, pensions and minimum-wage protection. Several countries are now testing a third legal category, between employee and contractor, to extend basic security without erasing flexibility.
questions.text: The 'third legal category' mentioned in the passage aims to:

options.text: Convert all gig workers into full employees
Give basic security while preserving flexibility

questions.explanation: It sits between employee and contractor status.

questions.text: Find the next term: 3, 10, 29, 66, 127, ?
Find the next term: 5, 7, 11, 19, 35, ?
questions.explanation: Differences double: 2, 4, 8, 16, then 32.

questions.text: Find the next term: 240, 120, 40, 10, 2, ?
questions.explanation: Divide successively by 2, 3, 4, 5, then 6.

questions.text: Find the next term: BEH, KNQ, TWZ, ?
questions.explanation: Each letter advances 9 places cyclically: T+9=C, W+9=F, Z+9=I.

questions.text: In the number 583962, what is the sum of the digits at even places minus the sum of the digits at odd places (counting from the left)?
If FRIEND is coded as GTLISJ, how is MOTHER coded?
If PEN = 35, then DESK = ?
In a code, 'give me water' = 'ho na pa', 'water is life' = 'na ja da' and 'life is precious' = 'da ja xi'. What is the code for 'precious'?
questions.explanation: 'da' and 'ja' are shared with sentence 2, leaving 'xi'.

questions.text: Six people L, M, N, O, P, Q sit in a row facing north. L sits third to the left of M, and neither L nor M is at an extreme end. O is immediately to the right of L, and P is immediately to the left of M. N sits somewhere to the left of L. Who sits at the extreme right?
questions.explanation: Order is N, L, O, P, M, Q.

questions.text: Eight children sit around a circle in the clockwise alphabetical order A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H. If D and G exchange seats, who now sits directly opposite D?
questions.explanation: D moves to G's seat (7th); opposite the 7th seat is the 3rd, C.

questions.text: In a row of boys, Ram is 8th from the left and Shyam is 12th from the right. When they interchange positions, Ram becomes 21st from the left. How many boys are in the row?
questions.explanation: Ram now occupies Shyam's seat: 21 + 12 - 1 = 32.

questions.text: The calendar of the leap year 2024 will be exactly repeated in which year?
questions.explanation: A leap year's calendar repeats after 28 years.

questions.text: Pointing to a boy in a photograph, a woman says, 'His brother's father is the only son of my grandfather.' How is the boy related to the woman?
questions.explanation: The only son of her grandfather is her father, who is also the boy's father.

questions.text: A * B means A is the wife of B; A ? B means A is the son of B; A $ B means A is the sister of B. In P ? Q * R, how is R related to P?
questions.explanation: P is Q's son and Q is R's wife, so R is P's father.

questions.text: At sunrise, Ram and Shyam stand talking face to face. Shyam's shadow falls exactly to his right. Which direction is Ram facing?
questions.explanation: Shadows fall west at sunrise; west is Shyam's right only if he faces south, so Ram faces north.

questions.text: Which pair of signs, inserted in order, makes the equation 18 ? 6 ? 3 = 9 correct?
Find the odd one out: AEI, BFJ, CGK, DHM
questions.explanation: The gap pattern +4, +4 breaks in DHM (D, H, M is +4, +5).

questions.text: Statements: All papers are files. All files are racks. No rack is a box. Conclusions: I. No paper is a box. II. All papers are racks.
questions.explanation: Papers are racks (chain), and racks exclude boxes.

questions.text: Three positions of the same dice are shown. Determine the number on the face opposite to 5.

figure.label: Dice Positions
figure.description: Three rotated views of a numbered dice from which the face opposite 5 must be deduced.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual dice question.

questions.text: A sheet is folded as shown and a shape is cut out. Select how the sheet appears when unfolded.

figure.label: Paper Folding & Cutting
figure.description: A sheet folded diagonally with a notch cut; the unfolded pattern must be chosen from four options.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual folding question.

questions.text: Which pair won India's first-ever Olympic medal in mixed team shooting (10 m air pistol) at Paris 2024?
questions.explanation: They took bronze in the 10 m air pistol mixed team event.

questions.text: The Nobel Prize in Economic Sciences 2024 (Acemoglu, Johnson, Robinson) was awarded for studies on:
questions.explanation: Their work links institutions to differences in prosperity.

questions.text: Which Indian Air Force officer was assigned as prime astronaut for the Axiom-4 mission to the International Space Station?
questions.explanation: Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla was named prime for Ax-4.

questions.text: 'Operation Greens' of the Government of India was originally launched to stabilise the prices of:
questions.explanation: It targeted the TOP crops: tomato, onion, potato.

questions.text: The Simon Commission, boycotted with black-flag protests, arrived in India in:
questions.explanation: It landed in Bombay in February 1928.

questions.text: The Treaty of Srirangapatna (1792) was signed at the end of which war?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan ceded half his territory after the Third war.

questions.text: Majuli, among the world's largest river islands, lies in which river?
questions.explanation: Majuli is in the Brahmaputra in Assam.

questions.text: Indira Point, India's southernmost tip, often in the news due to the Great Nicobar project, lies closest to which country?
questions.explanation: It is about 150 km from Indonesia's Sumatra.

questions.text: Theyyam, a ritual dance form with elaborate face painting, belongs to which region?
questions.explanation: Theyyam is performed in northern Kerala.

questions.text: The President's power to promulgate ordinances when Parliament is not in session comes from:
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President; 213 is for Governors.

questions.text: The International Booker Prize 2024 went to 'Kairos', written by:
questions.explanation: German author Jenny Erpenbeck won with translator Michael Hofmann.

questions.text: 'Jatiya Sangsad' is the national parliament of:
questions.explanation: Bangladesh's parliament sits in Dhaka.

questions.text: Who won the men's singles title at the Australian Open 2025?
questions.explanation: Sinner defended his title, beating Zverev in the final.

questions.text: 'NPS Vatsalya', launched in 2024, is a scheme that allows:

options.text: Pension accounts to be opened for minors

questions.explanation: Parents can open NPS accounts in a child's name.

questions.text: The 'World Economic Outlook' report is published by the:
questions.explanation: The IMF publishes the WEO twice a year.

questions.text: At the 46th World Heritage Committee session in New Delhi (2024), which Indian site was inscribed on the UNESCO list?
questions.explanation: The Ahom burial mounds of Charaideo were inscribed.

questions.text: In Excel, A1:A4 contain 10, 25, 30, 15. What does =SUMIF(A1:A4,">=20") return?
In Excel, the reference $A$1 is:
questions.explanation: Both row and column are locked with $.

questions.text: In MS Word, Ctrl + H opens which dialog?
questions.explanation: Ctrl + H opens Find and Replace.

questions.text: In Windows, Alt + F4 pressed on the desktop:
questions.explanation: It closes windows; on the desktop, it offers shutdown.

questions.text: Virtual memory is implemented by using:

options.text: A portion of the hard disk as an extension of RAM

questions.explanation: Disk space (page file/swap) extends physical memory.

questions.text: Which of the following memories is volatile?
questions.explanation: DRAM loses its contents without power.

questions.text: Spooling is a technique most commonly associated with:
questions.explanation: Print jobs are queued (spooled) on disk.

questions.text: Firmware is best described as:

options.text: Any application installed by the user
A physical component of the CPU

questions.explanation: Firmware lives in ROM/flash inside devices.

questions.text: The machine-language output produced by a compiler is called:
questions.explanation: Compilers turn source code into object code.

questions.text: The 'word length' of a computer refers to:

options.text: The number of characters in a file name
The number of bits the CPU can process at a time
The width of the monitor in pixels
The size of the hard disk

questions.explanation: A 64-bit machine processes 64-bit words.
